_README: Single source of truth for the models the cross-model verification / ULTRA pipeline uses. 'active' = the latest VALIDATED high-performance models (probed to work via the chat-completions / generateContent endpoints). 'reported' = the exact models that produced whitepaper/03-results.md — NEVER edit these retroactively; re-run the eval before re-citing numbers with newer models. Updates to 'active' go through orchestration/lib/model-freshness.mjs: detect (daily, model-list only) -> validate (eval gate) -> adopt. See orchestration/MODELS.md.

cost_routing._note: Task-category tier selection is a spend policy only. It does not assert output quality, model capability, accuracy, or task success. Each ordered category chain starts with its cost route and ends with its fallback.

openai.max_quality_note: gpt-5.5-pro is NOT a chat model (needs /v1/responses or /v1/completions); not used on the chat path until that endpoint is wired.

anthropic.note: Claude worker runs via the Workflow tool; 'opus' resolves to the latest Claude Opus available to Claude Code.

reported_in_whitepaper._note: These produced the published 18/18 result. Do not relabel; re-run to re-cite.

validation.gate: A candidate is adopted into 'active' only after (1) a live API probe confirms it works on the chat/generateContent endpoint, and (2) an eval run records its defect-catch vs the current pin (eval/ultra/ scripts on eval/fixtures/seeded-defects-hard.json), recorded in 'history'. Adoption favors the LATEST available high-performance model and does NOT require beating the prior peak recall: newer models can trade recall for precision (history shows gpt-5.5/gemini-3.1-pro-preview at 16/18 @ 0.74 vs the prior 18/18 @ 0.63). When that happens, BOTH runs are recorded here and the published whitepaper numbers keep the models that produced them (reported_in_whitepaper). The bar is validated-to-work + no catastrophic recall regression, with any tradeoff disclosed — never a silent swap.

history.event: active pins set to latest validated models (gpt-5.5, gemini-3.1-pro-preview) after live API probe; whitepaper numbers retain gpt-5.2 + gemini-2.5-pro as the reported peak run.

ULTRA re-run on active pins (gpt-5.5 adjudicator + 4x gpt-5.5 / 1x gemini-3.1-pro-preview panel): 16/18 recall (0.889), precision 0.742 — higher precision but ~2 fewer deep-reasoning catches than the gpt-5.2/gemini-2.5-pro peak (18/18, 0.631). Newest != higher recall on this n=6 single run. Recorded in whitepaper/03-results.md as V3.
